# Resource (hush_gcp_sa_access_credential)

Manage GCP SA dynamic access credentials in the Hush Security platform.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Create a GCP SA credential with a service account key
resource "hush_gcp_sa_access_credential" "example" {
  name                   = "prod-gcp-sa"
  description            = "Production GCP SA credential"
  deployment_ids         = [hush_deployment.example.id]
  service_account_key_wo = var.gcp_service_account_key
}

# Create a GCP SA credential with workload identity (no explicit key)
resource "hush_gcp_sa_access_credential" "workload_identity" {
  name           = "prod-gcp-sa-wif"
  description    = "GCP SA credential using workload identity"
  deployment_ids = [hush_deployment.example.id]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the GCP SA access credential
- `deployment_ids` (List of String) List of deployment IDs that can access this credential

### Optional

- `description` (String) The description of the GCP SA access credential
- `service_account_key` (String, Sensitive) The GCP SA key JSON
- `service_account_key_wo` (String, Sensitive, Write-only) The GCP SA key JSON (write-only). This is a write-only attribute that is more secure than `service_account_key` because Terraform will not store this value in the state file. Either `service_account_key` or `service_account_key_wo` must be specified.
- `service_account_key_wo_version` (String) Used to trigger updates for `service_account_key_wo`. This value should be changed when the service account key content changes. Can be any value (e.g., a timestamp, version number, or hash).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the GCP SA access credential
- `type` (String) The type of access credential
- `kind` (String) The kind of access credential
